content.problemStatement:
There are NNN types of medicines and MMM types of diseases where medicine types are numbered from 111 to NNN and disease types are numbered from 111 to MMM. You have **infinite** medicines of each type.

For every disease ddd, you have a strength f(d)f(d)f(d). Initially, f(d)=0f(d) = 0f(d)=0 for all 1≤d≤M1 \le d \le M1≤d≤M.

The ithi^{th}ith medicine increments your strength for a particular disease (say xix_ixi​) and decrements your strength for another disease (say yiy_iyi​).
In particular, if you take the medicine of ithi^{th}ith type, then:


f(xi)→f(xi)+1f(x_i) \to f(x_i) + 1f(xi​)→f(xi​)+1
f(yi)→f(yi)−1f(y_i) \to f(y_i) - 1f(yi​)→f(yi​)−1
Let SSS  be a subset of the MMM diseases. You are considered **immune** to SSS if and only if, there exists a finite number of medicines you can take such that after you take all those medicines, you have f(d)>0f(d) > 0f(d)>0  for all d∈Sd \in Sd∈S.

Remember that you have infinite medicines of each type. So you could take 101010 medicines of type 111, and 333 medicines of type 222, etc.

Count the number of subsets of diseases you are **immune** to. Print the number modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of types of medicines and the number of types of diseases, respectively.
The next NNN lines describe the medicines. The ithi^{th}ith of these NNN lines contain two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and yiy_iyi​, denoting the disease on which the strength increases and decreases respectively after taking the ithi^{th}ith medicine.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of subsets of diseases you are immune to, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: xi≠yix_i \neq y_ixi​=yi​ and the ordered pair (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi​,yi​) is pairwise distinct for 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.
The sum of N+MN + MN+M over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 444 subsets of diseases that you are immune to.

The {}\{\}{} set: there are no diseases so you are automatically immune to the empty set.
{2}\{2\}{2}: If you take the first tablet, you become immune.
{3}\{3\}{3}: If you take the second tablet, you become immune.
{2,3}\{2, 3\}{2,3}: If you take the first and second tablet then your, f(1)f(1)f(1) becomes −2-2−2, f(2)f(2)f(2) becomes 111 and f(3)f(3)f(3) becomes 111. Which makes you immune to this subset.

It can be proven that you are not immune to any other subset.
